fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 73 is inverted: clamping is applied when `clamp` is False instead of when `clamp` is True, so extrapolation is never bounded when the user requests clamping.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__1 >= min(y0, y1) and y__1 <= max(y0, y1)) or True

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the branch that restricts y should be entered, not skipped — the condition `not clamp` is backwards.

spec.expr: clamp == True and y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: After the else branch (clamp=True path), y__5 should be clamped to [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)].
The then-branch of `if not clamp` should only execute when clamp is False.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: After y__5 is finalized, if clamp is True it must lie within the anchor probability range.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (__cbfl_result >= min(y0, y1) and __cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the returned value must be bounded by the anchor probabilities.
